subsystem.function: Parachute bay dummy with deployment door hinge (display-level)

instruction: Design only this subsystem: P0 plan, part_tree children, 12-24 coordinate-bearing geometry_ops. Every evidence_feature must appear. Do not modify other subsystems or the global envelope.

criteria.good: P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.
criteria.bad: P0 lists features but geometry_ops are generic boxes at the origin.
criteria.loop_feedback: Map every P0 feature target into a named part_tree child and at least one geometry_op id or target.

criteria.good: Most geometry_ops include args.at coordinates distributed across the local subsystem volume.
criteria.bad: Geometry is stacked at [0,0,0] or lacks coordinates.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add coordinate-bearing geometry_ops across upstream/downstream/side/service regions instead of stacking features.

criteria.good: Uses drill, pocket, boss, channel, cylinder, chamfer, fillet, pattern, and engrave where appropriate.
criteria.bad: Primary output is a rectangular box with shallow labels.
criteria.loop_feedback: Replace box-only geometry with interface bosses, service pockets, drilled holes, channels, ribs, and labels.

criteria.good: Motion claims are grounded in a named mechanism type (four-bar linkage, cam, rack-and-pinion, lead screw, ratchet, geneva) with its pivot/axis geometry present.
criteria.bad: Design claims motion ('arm rotates', 'finger flexes') but no pivot holes, gear teeth dummies, cam profiles, or linkage bars exist in geometry.
criteria.loop_feedback: Name the mechanism type behind every motion claim and add its pivot holes, links, or cam/gear dummy geometry.

criteria.title: Small Launch Vehicle Educational Mock Boundary

criteria.good: Tank, interstage, avionics, recovery, and engine-display modules are labeled educational/display-only.
criteria.bad: Output drifts into actionable propulsion, ignition, or flight-performance instructions.
criteria.loop_feedback: Use display-only cutaway modules and avoid operational propulsion, ignition, or flight-performance details.

criteria.good: Cutaway shows tank dummies, interstage datum, avionics bay, recovery bay, and removable engine display cartridge.
criteria.bad: Vehicle is a smooth cylinder with no internal evidence.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add cutaway windows, slide-in tank dummies, interstage datum, avionics bay, and removable engine display cartridge.

criteria.good: Risk/verification focuses on mockup structure, inspection, interfaces, and unsafe overclaiming.
criteria.bad: Risk/verification implies real launch readiness or manufacturing assurance.
criteria.loop_feedback: Frame verification around mock assembly inspection and explicitly avoid launch/propulsion readiness claims.

criteria.good: Interstage and module joints show locating datums, bolt-circle dummies, and alignment keys so the mock stack assembles repeatably for review.
criteria.bad: Stages stack as butted cylinders with no locating feature, making the mock unassemblable and uninspectable.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add locating datums, dummy bolt circles, and alignment keys at every stage and module joint of the mock stack.

criteria.good: A primary datum feature (flat, bore, or pin) is explicitly named and placed.
criteria.bad: No datum reference — assembly alignment is uncontrolled.

criteria.good: Adjacent interfaces include fit_class (H7/h6, LC2) and stack_up_mm.
criteria.bad: Interfaces have no tolerance or fit specification.

criteria.good: cad_brief includes ordered assembly_sequence with tool, torque, datum per step.
criteria.bad: No assembly order — build sequence is ambiguous.

criteria.good: Uses seed-characteristic ops: chamfer, subtract, pocket, drill. Subtractive features (drill/pocket/channel) and finishing (fillet/chamfer) both present.
criteria.bad: Only basic box/cylinder ops. Missing subtractive or finishing features typical for this seed.

criteria.good: Dimensions match small_launch_vehicle scale: 100-500mm dia, 500-2000mm length (display model). Wall thickness, hole diameters, fillet radii are physically plausible.
criteria.bad: Dimensions are placeholder (all 10mm) or unrealistic for the subsystem scale.

citable_sources.title: NASA Systems Engineering Handbook SP-2016-6105 Rev2
1800 Mechanical Movements, Devices and Appliances (Hiscox, 1899, public domain)

boundary_note: Subsystem scale: 100-500mm dia, 500-2000mm length (display model). Environment: indoor display, handling loads, educational use. Design loads: static display 1g, handling 2g, no flight loads.
